mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is being cut. The signal is directional only when the rate is in a downward move rather than flat.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 25 bps over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show higher 20d SPX returns than non-triggered observations, or if the triggered win rate is below 55% over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent trend indicates weakening household income and lower earnings expectations, which should pressure equity prices over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment is clearly deteriorating rather than stable.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX forward 20d returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the triggered hit rate for negative returns falls below 55% in any 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and revenue momentum, which supports equity earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is positive when sales are expanding faster than the recent baseline.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive RSAFS gains are followed by negative or flat 20d SPX performance more often than positive performance, or if the conditional mean return is not above unconditional mean by at least 0.5 percentage points annualized.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO with stable labor conditions signals improving real output without immediate overheating, supporting corporate margins and cyclical equity performance over the next quarter. The mechanism fails when output growth is paired with inflationary policy tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes do not predict higher 60d SPX returns when UNRATE is flat or falling, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus INDPRO alone in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is high relative to recent history and CPIAUCSL is still rising, the market discounts further tightening and lower valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is activated only in a restrictive inflationary regime.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation is rising
failure_condition: Reject if the combined restrictive-inflation condition does not precede negative 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional average return is not materially below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals weakening housing demand and construction activity, which foreshadows slower broad economic growth and weaker equity returns over the following quarter. The effect is strongest when housing starts are falling for multiple months in a row.
failure_condition: Reject if repeated HOUST declines are followed by positive 60d SPX returns more frequently than negative returns, or if the signal loses significance after accounting for recent market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE indicates broad labor-market improvement, which supports consumption and earnings expectations and should lift the S&P 500 over the next 20 trading days. The effect requires both indicators to move in the same favorable direction.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines do not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20d SPX returns, or if the conditional win rate stays below 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO with stable CPIAUCSL in a post-shock recovery indicates real activity is improving without an inflation squeeze, which supports equity multiples and earnings over the next 60 trading days. The signal is strongest after periods of industrial weakness.
failure_condition: Reject if rising INDPRO with stable CPIAUCSL fails to predict above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive and significant in rolling walk-forward tests.
